meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Setup: Master Debugging for AI Students
content.seo_description: Learn to master Claude Code skills to eliminate debugging hurdles. A high-impact, intermediate guide for students building their first AI-powered SaaS apps.
content.answer_block: To overcome persistent debugging blocks, integrate Claude Code into your local environment. By configuring Claude to analyze your stack, you transform abstract errors into actionable code fixes. This setup bridges the gap between frustration and production-ready software, allowing you to iterate faster and build professional-grade applications with confidence.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal flooded with stack traces, your heart rate spiking as the 'submit' deadline looms. You’ve refactored the logic three times, yet the bug remains—a silent, stubborn shadow in your codebase. This isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of leverage. Every senior developer knows that the difference between a junior and a lead isn't knowing the answer, but knowing how to interrogate the system until it reveals the truth.

Today, we stop guessing. We are deploying Claude Code, an AI-native CLI tool that acts as your pair programmer, coupled with Antigravity to streamline your workflow. We’re moving beyond simple syntax fixes into architectural debugging. By the time you finish this guide, you won't just solve your current bug; you'll have a systematic approach to tearing down any technical wall you encounter in your dev journey.

steps.title: Environment Initialization
steps.description: Establish a clean, isolated environment to ensure Claude Code has full context of your project structure without noise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a clean development workspace scanning the local directory for dependency conflicts and environment variable mismatches.

steps.title: Context Loading and Indexing
steps.description: Feed your project's architectural patterns into the AI so it understands your specific coding style and framework constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Index the current project structure and map dependencies to provide Claude with a high-level architectural overview.

steps.title: Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Use Claude to perform a deep-dive trace of your error logs to identify the exact point of failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided stack trace and correlate it with the recent git diffs to identify the regression point.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Analyze the error in logs/debug.log and pinpoint the source file causing the crash.'

steps.title: Automated Refactoring
steps.description: Generate a solution that adheres to your established coding standards while fixing the bug.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a refactored code block for the identified bug that adheres to DRY principles and includes unit tests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/main.py --instruction 'Fix the null pointer exception while maintaining the current API structure.'

steps.title: Validation and Regression Testing
steps.description: Verify the fix is robust by running automated tests against the new code changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run the test suite and verify that the fix addresses the bug without introducing side effects in peripheral modules.

pro_tips: Always use the --verbose flag in Claude Code when debugging complex race conditions to see the AI's reasoning chain.
Create a .claudeignore file to prevent the AI from indexing massive node_modules or build directories, which saves context window space.
Use Antigravity to generate 'unit test scenarios' before running the fix to ensure the bug is actually squashed.
If you hit a wall, ask Claude to 'explain the architectural implications' of its proposed fix before applying the edit.
Commit your code after every successful Claude-assisted fix to create a granular history you can revert to if the AI hallucinates.
content.next_steps: Now that your debugging environment is optimized, start documenting your logic flows in a Notion or Obsidian vault to build your personal knowledge base for future projects.
content.conversion_block: You now have the power to dismantle any bug standing between you and your MVP. Stop wrestling with errors and start shipping features that change the world. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
